postsNeedingUpdates.title: Building a Coffee Data Pipeline from Scratch
reasons: Still claims 31 suppliers, 12 live suppliers, and 1,876 coffees across 35 suppliers
Architecture mix has drifted from the published 5 Playwright / 7 generic Shopify split
Current product narrative has moved to a 41+ supplier surface
postsNeedingUpdates.suggestedFix: Refresh counts throughout or explicitly label the post as a February 2026 snapshot.

postsNeedingUpdates.title: What is Purveyors?
reasons: Uses 35 suppliers twice in present-tense product copy
Current public surfaces have moved beyond that supplier count
postsNeedingUpdates.suggestedFix: Update to a 40+ / 41+ supplier narrative or time-scope the count.

postsNeedingUpdates.title: The Real AI Moats Aren't Software
reasons: Moat example still cites a normalized dataset across 35 suppliers
postsNeedingUpdates.suggestedFix: Update to the current supplier narrative or generalize to 40+ suppliers.

postsNeedingUpdates.title: Who Profits When Coffee Data Stays Scarce?
reasons: Body still presents a 30-supplier dataset snapshot without clearly date-scoping it

postsNeedingUpdates.title: The Codebase Is the Onboarding Manual

newIdeas.title: The listing schema is the market: why a scraped catalog should become a publishing standard
newIdeas.whyNow: Recent coffee-app strategy work created a concrete, post-worthy thesis about moving from scraping as the moat to schema ownership as the moat.

newIdeas.title: Retry budgets are data quality policy: rate limits, network flaps, and upstream failures should not share the same counter
newIdeas.whyNow: Last week's scraper changes exposed a strong general lesson about treating retry classes as product policy, not incidental transport logic.

staleIdeas.title: Is Co-Fermentation Cheating? Wrong Question.
staleIdeas.reason: Aging drafted post. Before shipping, the processing-coverage claims should be rerun against the current supplier surface.

staleIdeas.title: Why Does the Same Coffee Cost $20/lb and $6.58/lb?
staleIdeas.reason: Aging drafted post. The tier-spread evidence likely needs a fresh data pass or a conscious archive decision.

publicationCadence.notes: Cadence slipped after the 2026-04-07 post. No post shipped during the week ending 2026-04-19.

notes: Status hygiene improved this audit by reconciling published idea entries for Sycophancy, Product Philosophy, Enterprise Second Brains, and the Who Profits draft lineage.
Several stale supplier-count idea titles were rewritten into timeless backlog entries instead of frozen historical counts.
The main backlog weakness is not idea volume. It is pillar imbalance and two aging drafted posts.

nextPriority.title: Can an agent use your API without help?
rationale: api-architecture still has zero published posts
Recent anonymous catalog hardening, docs canonicalization, llms.txt work, and teaser pagination gave the outline fresh shipped evidence
It aligns directly with PRODUCT_VISION principles around API-first product strategy and public surfaces proving value before the paywall
